recipes.title: Sheet Pan Tofu & Veggie Teriyaki
recipes.description: Crispy baked tofu with a medley of vegetables in a light teriyaki glaze, all roasted on one pan for an easy high-protein vegetarian dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a large sheet pan with parchment paper.
Press the tofu for 10–15 minutes, then cut into 1-inch cubes.
Chop the broccoli, bell pepper, carrot, and red onion into bite-size pieces.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, maple syrup, rice vinegar, minced garlic, grated ginger, and cornstarch.
Toss tofu cubes with half of the sauce and spread on one side of the sheet pan. Toss vegetables with olive oil and the remaining sauce, then spread on the other side.
Bake for 25–30 minutes, flipping tofu and stirring vegetables halfway, until tofu is golden and edges are crisp.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds and serve hot over rice or quinoa if desired.

recipes.title: Lentil & Spinach Curry with Brown Rice
recipes.description: Creamy red lentil and spinach curry simmered in coconut-tomato sauce, served over nutty brown rice for a satisfying vegetarian dinner.
instructions: Rinse lentils under cold water until the water runs clear.
Heat olive oil in a pot over medium heat. Add diced onion and sauté 3–4 minutes until softened.
Stir in minced garlic, grated ginger, curry powder, and cumin, and cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Add lentils, diced tomatoes, vegetable broth, and coconut milk. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer 18–20 minutes until lentils are tender.
Stir in spinach and cook 2–3 minutes until wilted. Adjust salt and pepper to taste.
Serve curry over bowls of warm cooked brown rice.

recipes.title: High-Protein Veggie Stir-Fry with Edamame
recipes.description: Colorful stir-fried vegetables with shelled edamame in a garlic-ginger sauce, ready in under 30 minutes.
instructions: Prep the vegetables: slice bell pepper and carrot, trim snap peas, and chop green onions.
In a small bowl, whisk soy sauce, minced garlic, grated ginger, cornstarch, and 2 tablespoons of water.
Heat neutral oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add carrots and bell pepper, stir-frying 2–3 minutes, then add snap peas and edamame.
Pour in the sauce and cook, stirring, for 3–4 minutes until thickened and vegetables are crisp-tender.
Finish with sesame oil and half the green onions. Garnish with remaining green onions and serve over rice or noodles.

recipes.title: Mushroom & Quinoa Protein Pilaf
recipes.description: Hearty quinoa pilaf with sautéed mushrooms, peas, and toasted almonds for a comforting vegetarian dinner.
instructions: Rinse quinoa in a fine-mesh sieve and drain well.
Add quinoa and vegetable broth to a saucepan, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer 15 minutes until liquid is absorbed.
Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a skillet. Sauté diced onion 3 minutes, then add sliced mushrooms and cook until browned and moisture evaporates.
Stir in minced garlic, peas, salt, and pepper. Cook 2–3 more minutes.
Fluff cooked quinoa with a fork and combine with the mushroom mixture.
Toast almonds in a dry pan 2–3 minutes until golden. Stir into pilaf along with chopped parsley and serve warm.

recipes.title: Paneer Tikka Power Bowls
recipes.description: Marinated paneer baked with peppers and onions, served over spiced quinoa with yogurt sauce.
instructions: Whisk yogurt, lemon juice, minced garlic, garam masala, cumin, paprika, salt, and olive oil in a bowl.
Cut paneer into cubes and toss in the marinade. Chill at least 20 minutes.
Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
Chop bell pepper and onion into chunks and spread on a parchment-lined baking sheet with marinated paneer.
Bake 18–20 minutes, turning once, until paneer edges are golden.
Assemble bowls with warm quinoa, roasted paneer and veggies, diced cucumber, and chopped cilantro. Drizzle with extra yogurt if desired.

recipes.title: Baked Salmon with Asparagus & Lemon
recipes.description: Simple sheet pan salmon with asparagus, garlic, and lemon wedges for a quick high-protein pescatarian dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Trim the woody ends from asparagus and spread on the baking sheet.
Place salmon fillets skin-side down among the asparagus.
Drizzle everything with olive oil, then season with minced garlic, salt, pepper, dill, and paprika.
Slice lemon and lay a few slices over salmon, with remaining slices scattered on the pan.
Bake 12–15 minutes, until salmon flakes easily with a fork and asparagus is tender-crisp.

recipes.title: Chicken Buddha Bowl with Veggies
recipes.description: Balanced grain bowl with grilled chicken, roasted vegetables, and tahini dressing.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Dice sweet potato and toss with 1 tablespoon olive oil, salt, pepper, and smoked paprika.
Spread sweet potato and broccoli on a baking sheet and roast 20–25 minutes, stirring halfway.
Season chicken breast with salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Grill or pan-sear 5–6 minutes per side until cooked through, then slice.
In a small bowl, whisk tahini, lemon juice, water, and a pinch of salt until smooth.
Assemble bowls with quinoa, spinach, roasted vegetables, and sliced chicken. Drizzle with tahini dressing.

recipes.title: Black Bean Taco Bowls
recipes.description: Flavor-packed taco bowls with spiced black beans, corn, and salsa for an easy vegan dinner.
instructions: Warm black beans in a small saucepan with chili powder, cumin, olive oil, and salt for 3–4 minutes.
Chop lettuce and dice avocado.
Divide brown rice between serving bowls.
Top with seasoned black beans, corn, lettuce, salsa, and avocado.
Drizzle with lime juice before serving.

recipes.title: Overnight Oats with Berries & Protein
recipes.description: Creamy overnight oats mixed with Greek yogurt, chia seeds, and protein powder, topped with fresh berries.
instructions: In a jar or container, combine oats, almond milk, Greek yogurt, protein powder, chia seeds, maple syrup, vanilla, and a pinch of salt.
Stir until well combined, then cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.
In the morning, stir the oats and add a splash of milk if needed for desired consistency.
Top with fresh berries just before serving.

recipes.title: Vegan Tofu Scramble on Avocado Toast
recipes.description: Turmeric-spiced tofu scramble piled on creamy avocado toast for a plant-based breakfast.
instructions: Crumble tofu into a bowl and mix with nutritional yeast, turmeric, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat, add tofu mixture, and cook 5–6 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in spinach and halved cherry tomatoes and cook until spinach wilts.
Toast bread slices and mash avocado on top.
Pile tofu scramble over avocado toast and serve immediately.

recipes.title: High-Protein Spinach & Feta Omelet
recipes.description: Fluffy three-egg omelet packed with spinach, feta, and herbs for a satisfying breakfast.
instructions: Whisk eggs, egg whites, milk, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
Heat olive oil in a nonstick skillet over medium heat.
Add spinach and sauté 1–2 minutes until wilted.
Pour in egg mixture and cook, gently lifting the edges, until mostly set.
Sprinkle feta and dill over half the omelet, fold, and cook 1 more minute until set in the center.

recipes.title: Mediterranean Chickpea Salad
recipes.description: Fresh chickpea salad with cucumber, tomato, olives, and a lemon-herb dressing.
instructions: Chop cucumber, halve cherry tomatoes, thinly slice red onion, and chop parsley.
In a large bowl, combine chickpeas, cucumber, tomatoes, onion, and olives.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, salt, and pepper in a small bowl.
Pour dressing over the salad, add parsley, and toss to combine.
Chill for at least 15 minutes before serving for best flavor.

recipes.title: Turkey & Hummus Veggie Wrap
recipes.description: Whole-wheat wrap filled with sliced turkey, hummus, and crunchy vegetables for a portable lunch.
instructions: Warm the tortilla slightly in a dry pan to make it pliable.
Spread hummus evenly over the tortilla.
Layer turkey slices, mixed greens, carrots, bell pepper, and cucumber down the center.
Season lightly with salt and pepper.
Roll up tightly, tucking in the sides as you go, then slice in half to serve.

recipes.title: Quinoa & Roasted Veggie Lunch Bowl
recipes.description: Warm quinoa topped with roasted seasonal vegetables and a lemon-tahini drizzle.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Dice sweet potato, zucchini, and onion.
Toss vegetables with 1 tablespoon olive oil, salt, and pepper, then spread on a baking sheet and roast 20–25 minutes.
Rinse quinoa and cook with vegetable broth according to package directions.
In a small bowl, whisk tahini, lemon juice, minced garlic, water, and a pinch of salt until smooth.
Divide quinoa into bowls, top with roasted vegetables, and drizzle with tahini sauce.

recipes.title: Peanut Butter Banana Protein Bites
recipes.description: No-bake snack bites made with oats, peanut butter, banana, and protein powder for an easy grab-and-go snack.
instructions: Mash the banana in a mixing bowl.
Add peanut butter, honey, protein powder, and oats. Stir until a thick dough forms.
Fold in dark chocolate chips.
Roll into 1-inch balls and place on a parchment-lined tray.
Chill for at least 20 minutes before serving. Store in the fridge.

recipes.title: Greek Yogurt Berry Parfait Cups
recipes.description: Layered parfait cups with Greek yogurt, berries, and crunchy granola for a protein-rich snack.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix Greek yogurt with honey, vanilla, and lemon zest.
Spoon a layer of yogurt into the bottom of small cups or jars.
Add a layer of berries and a sprinkle of granola.
Repeat layers until cups are filled, finishing with berries, granola, and chia seeds.
Serve immediately or chill up to 24 hours.

recipes.title: Roasted Chickpea Crunch Snack
recipes.description: Crispy oven-roasted chickpeas tossed with spices for a high-fiber, budget-friendly snack.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Pat chickpeas dry with a paper towel.
Toss chickpeas with olive oil, smoked paprika, garlic powder, and salt.
Spread in a single layer on a baking sheet.
Roast for 25–30 minutes, shaking the pan halfway, until crisp and golden.
Cool slightly before serving for maximum crunch.

recipes.title: Dark Chocolate Nut Trail Mix
recipes.description: Homemade trail mix with nuts, seeds, and dark chocolate chunks for an energy-boosting snack.
instructions: Combine all nuts, seeds, dried fruits, and coconut flakes in a large bowl.
Stir in dark chocolate chunks and a pinch of sea salt.
Mix until evenly distributed.
Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 weeks.

workouts.equipment_notes: Pair of light dumbbells or filled water bottles.

workouts.name: Morning Mobility Flow

workout_plans.name: Beginner Full-Body 3x/Week

workout_plans.name: Fat Loss Cardio Blend

workout_plans.name: Endurance 5K Builder
workout_plans.description: Six-week progressive running plan to build endurance toward a comfortable 5K.

meals.label: Lunch

meals.label: Dinner

meals.label: Snack

meals.label: Lunch

meals.label: Dinner

meals.label: Lunch

meals.label: Snack

meals.label: Lunch

meals.label: Dinner

meals.label: Dinner

meal_plans.name: Breakfast Power Start 7-Day Plan
meal_plans.description: A 7-day breakfast-focused meal plan built around protein-rich morning meals like overnight oats and omelets, with balanced lunches and dinners to support general health and energy.

meal_plans.name: Breakfast Fiber Boost 7-Day Plan
meal_plans.description: A 7-day breakfast-heavy plan emphasizing high-fiber, high-protein morning meals to support appetite control, digestive health, and steady energy throughout the day.

meal_plans.name: Mini 3-Day Weight-Loss Kickstart
meal_plans.description: A short 3-day, calorie-controlled meal plan designed to gently reduce intake while keeping protein high and meals satisfying.

meal_plans.name: 3-Day Vegan Reset
meal_plans.description: A simple 3-day plant-based plan using familiar vegan breakfasts, hearty legume-based lunches, and balanced dinners to reset eating habits.

meal_plans.name: Balanced General Nutrition 14-Day Plan
meal_plans.description: A 14-day flexible plan featuring a mix of breakfasts, lunches, dinners, and snacks that balance protein, carbs, and fats for overall health without strict dieting.

meal_plans.name: Muscle Gain Basics 28-Day Plan
meal_plans.description: A 4-week higher-calorie, high-protein meal plan tailored to support beginner strength programs and lean muscle gain.

meal_plans.name: Mediterranean Heart-Healthy 21-Day Plan
meal_plans.description: A 3-week plan inspired by Mediterranean eating patterns, emphasizing vegetables, legumes, whole grains, and healthy fats to support heart health and cholesterol management.

meal_plans.name: Intermittent Fasting Companion 14-Day Plan
meal_plans.description: A 14-day meal structure designed to pair with popular intermittent fasting schedules, focusing on nutrient-dense meals during eating windows.

meal_plans.name: Quick-Prep 7-Day Weight-Loss Plan
meal_plans.description: A one-week low-effort weight-loss plan using recipes that can be prepped in under 30 minutes, ideal for busy schedules.

meal_plans.name: High-Protein Veggie Muscle 14-Day Plan
meal_plans.description: A vegetarian, high-protein 14-day plan created to support muscle gain with plant-forward meals that still meet protein needs.

content_items.title: 20-Minute Home Workout With No Equipment
content_items.summary: A fast-paced, full-body home workout that uses only bodyweight moves and fits perfectly into a busy morning.
content_items.body: This 20-minute routine strings together low-impact but effective bodyweight exercises to target your legs, core, and upper body without any equipment. After a short warm-up, you rotate through circuits of squats, lunges, push-ups, and core moves with built-in rest so beginners can keep up. Follow along in real time, modify as needed, and repeat the workout 3 times per week to build strength and endurance at home.

content_items.title: 30-Minute Low-Impact Home Cardio (No Equipment)
content_items.summary: Joint-friendly, zero-equipment cardio routine you can do in your living room to boost daily step count and endurance.
content_items.body: This low-impact session alternates marching, side steps, gentle squats, and easy boxing drills so you can raise your heart rate without jumping. The follow-along format includes clear cues for breathing, posture, and pacing. It is ideal for beginners, people returning from a break, or anyone needing a quiet apartment-friendly workout.

content_items.title: Intermittent Fasting 16:8 for Complete Beginners
content_items.summary: Learn how to start the 16:8 intermittent fasting method safely, including who it is for, what to eat, and how to handle hunger.
content_items.body: The 16:8 method is one of the simplest ways to try intermittent fasting: you fast for 16 hours and eat within an 8-hour window each day. This beginner guide walks you through choosing your eating window, building balanced meals that keep you full, staying hydrated, and avoiding common mistakes like over-restricting or overeating at night. It also outlines who should avoid fasting or talk with a doctor first, such as people with a history of disordered eating or certain medical conditions.

content_items.title: Beginner’s Guide to Intermittent Fasting Schedules (12:12, 14:10, 16:8)
content_items.summary: Compare the most popular intermittent fasting schedules and find the one that fits your lifestyle and experience level.
content_items.body: Not every fasting schedule works for every person. This guide explains the differences between 12:12, 14:10, and 16:8 fasting, including how they affect hunger, energy, and social life. You will learn how to pick a starting schedule, when to move to a longer fast, and how to structure meals around workouts or night shifts. Sample day plans show what to eat at each meal to maintain steady energy and support your goals.

content_items.title: What to Eat During Your Intermittent Fasting Window
content_items.summary: Focus on nutrient-dense meals during your eating window to avoid energy crashes and overeating later.
content_items.body: Intermittent fasting is not just about when you eat but also what you eat. In this article, you will learn how to build meals around protein, fiber, and healthy fats so you feel steady energy throughout your fasting schedule. We cover example meal templates, hydration tips, and how to time your workouts relative to your eating window for best performance and recovery.

content_items.title: 7-Day Cholesterol-Friendly Meal Plan
content_items.summary: A practical one-week meal plan built around fiber-rich plants, healthy fats, and lean proteins to help lower LDL cholesterol.
content_items.body: This 7-day sample meal plan shows you exactly what a cholesterol-friendly week of eating can look like. Each day includes breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snacks that emphasize oats, beans, nuts, seeds, fruits, vegetables, and oily fish while limiting saturated fat and refined sugars. Use the plan as written or mix and match meals to build a sustainable routine you enjoy.

content_items.title: 10 Foods That Help Lower Cholesterol Naturally
content_items.summary: Discover evidence-backed foods that can help reduce LDL cholesterol and support better heart health in under three months.
content_items.body: Certain foods have been shown to lower LDL cholesterol when eaten consistently. This article highlights oats, barley, beans, lentils, nuts, seeds, soy foods, leafy greens, berries, and extra-virgin olive oil. For each one, you will learn how it works, ideal serving sizes, and easy ways to add it to breakfast, lunch, dinner, or snacks.

content_items.title: How to Lower Cholesterol in 3 Months With Food First
content_items.summary: Step-by-step nutrition changes you can make over 12 weeks to reduce cholesterol numbers before relying on supplements or medications.
content_items.body: Lowering cholesterol is rarely about a single superfood. Instead, consistent dietary patterns over 8–12 weeks can make a measurable difference. This guide outlines a three-phase approach: replacing saturated fats with unsaturated fats, increasing soluble fiber intake, and reducing added sugars and ultra-processed foods. Sample grocery lists and weekly structures help you apply the plan in real life.

content_items.title: Cardio Workouts to Help Lower Cholesterol
content_items.summary: Learn how much and what type of cardio you need each week to meaningfully improve your cholesterol levels.
content_items.body: Regular aerobic exercise can boost HDL (the "good" cholesterol) and lower triglycerides, both of which support a healthier lipid profile. This article breaks down the research-backed weekly targets for moderate and vigorous cardio and offers sample walking, cycling, and jogging routines. A 12-week progression plan shows you how to gradually build from short walks to longer, more structured workouts.

content_items.title: Strength Training for Heart Health and Cholesterol
content_items.summary: Discover how 2–3 days of strength training per week can complement cardio to improve cholesterol and metabolic health.
content_items.body: While cardio gets most of the spotlight for heart health, strength training also contributes to better cholesterol, blood sugar control, and overall metabolic health. This guide explains how building muscle mass supports long-term heart health, outlines sample full-body routines using dumbbells or body weight, and shows you how to combine strength and cardio into a sustainable weekly schedule.

content_items.title: How Our Fitness Level Quiz Works (And What to Do With Your Result)
content_items.summary: Understand the science and scoring behind the fitness level quiz so you can use your result to set smarter goals.
content_items.body: Our fitness level quiz looks at your current workout frequency, intensity, and goals to suggest a starting point that is safe but challenging. In this article, we walk through each question and what your answer means, explain how your responses feed into recommendations such as "Improve endurance" or "Build strength," and share next steps like choosing a workout plan or updating your profile goal.

content_items.title: Using the Calorie Calculator to Find Your Maintenance Level
content_items.summary: Step-by-step instructions for using the calorie calculator and saving your baseline maintenance calories to your profile.
content_items.body: Your maintenance calories are an estimate of how much you can eat to keep your weight stable. This article explains what information to enter into the calorie calculator, how activity levels affect your result, and how to interpret the output. You will also learn how to save labeled results like "Baseline 2026-03" so you can compare changes over time as your weight, activity, or goals shift.

content_items.title: 5 High-Protein Vegetarian Dinners Under 600 Calories
content_items.summary: A curated list of vegetarian dinner recipes that keep calories in check while delivering at least 20 grams of protein per serving.
content_items.body: Eating vegetarian does not mean skimping on protein. This roundup features sheet-pan tofu teriyaki, lentil and spinach curry, high-protein veggie stir-fry, mushroom quinoa pilaf, and paneer tikka bowls. For each recipe, we highlight the key protein sources, approximate macros, and simple swaps you can make if you are cooking for one, feeding a family, or avoiding certain ingredients.

content_items.title: Should You Try a Breakfast-Focused Meal Plan?
content_items.summary: Explore the pros and cons of eating more of your daily calories at breakfast and how our breakfast-heavy plans are structured.
content_items.body: Front-loading your calories earlier in the day may help manage hunger, support blood sugar control, and make evening eating more relaxed. This article reviews the research on breakfast timing, explains how our Breakfast Power Start and Breakfast Fiber Boost meal plans are structured, and helps you decide whether a breakfast-heavy approach fits your lifestyle and preferences.

content_items.title: 10-Minute Morning Mobility Routine
content_items.summary: Gentle follow-along mobility session to loosen stiff hips, shoulders, and spine before your day starts.
content_items.body: This short guided routine moves your body through a series of controlled circles, reaches, and stretches to improve joint range of motion. It is perfect as a warm-up before a walk or strength workout, or as a standalone practice on active rest days. No equipment is required beyond a mat or soft surface.
